claim: All active citation keys resolve to unique BibTeX entries.
assessment: 70 citation occurrences resolve to 44 unique keys; missing keys=0; duplicate BibTeX keys=0; duplicate DOI groups=0.

claim: Every cited journal/method entry has a checked DOI or authoritative metadata record.
citation_keys: Ehlert2021ALPB; Bussi2007VRescale; Bernetti2020CRescale; Essmann1995PME; Savitzky1964; Park2021; Tichter2020
assessment: All 44 cited entries have DOI fields. The five strict method additions were separately checked against Crossref; Park2021 and Tichter2020 were checked against publisher/KCI and Refubium records.

claim: ZIFB flow-battery development is supported by directly relevant flow-battery literature.
assessment: The sentence is bounded to flow-battery background and does not recast static Zn-I2 work as direct ZIFB evidence.

claim: I2/I-/I3- equilibrium and soluble polyiodide representation are source-supported.
assessment: The cited sources support equilibrium/speciation context; Palmer is not used as evidence for an unmeasured kinetic rate.

claim: Confined carbon hosts can retain reversible solid iodine under host-specific conditions.
assessment: The manuscript preserves the host-specific boundary and does not extrapolate the cited morphology to the present felt.

claim: A dense iodine film can be associated with increased charge-transfer resistance in other conditions.
assessment: Wording is associative and cross-system; no numerical threshold is transplanted to the present cell.

claim: Charge/discharge morphology changes have been observed in another iodine battery chemistry.
assessment: The cited system is explicitly labeled as a different iodine cell and is not treated as identification of the present felt morphology.

claim: Classical porous-electrode theory and later flow-cell extensions support the modeling hierarchy.
assessment: Foundational theory and flow-battery extensions are stated in separate layers.

claim: Solid-product electrodes provide analogue evidence that precipitation can alter active area and transport.
assessment: The manuscript no longer labels all cited experimental/operando studies as models and limits the inference to an analogue.

claim: The adopted iodide-complexation coefficient is a conditional literature-scale prior.
assessment: 0.72 m3 mol-1 equals 720 M-1 and is described as near 698 +/- 10 M-1, not as an exact reproduction.

claim: The adopted bromide coefficient is near a reported I2Br- formation scale, while other papers establish only formation precedent.
assessment: 0.0115 m3 mol-1 equals 11.5 L mol-1 and is near Lee's 12.2 L mol-1; Park/Weng are not used for that number.

claim: Dilute-water I2 solubility and the salting multiplier are correctly separated.
assessment: 1.33 mol m-3 is a dilute-water reference. gamma_salt=3, varied 2-4, is explicitly a cross-salt prior rather than a measurement in the present electrolyte.

claim: CP2K, PBE, D3(BJ), MOLOPT, GTH and counterpoise methods have complete primary-method citations.
citation_keys: Kuhne2020CP2K; VandeVondele2005Quickstep; Perdew1996PBE; Grimme2010D3; Grimme2011D3BJ; VandeVondele2007MOLOPT; Goedecker1996GTH; Hartwigsen1998GTH; Krack2005GTHPBE; Boys1970Counterpoise
assessment: The manuscript also bounds the outputs as finite dry calculations, not solution free energies or nucleation barriers.

claim: GROMACS, SPC/E, ion parameters, Packmol and ECC are cited at first methodological use.
assessment: Standard method citations are not presented as validation of project-specific polyhalide analogue parameters.

claim: GFN2-xTB and ALPB-water have separate primary-method citations.
assessment: The absolute polyhalide parameterization remains labeled high-uncertainty.

claim: V-rescale, C-rescale and PME are cited where used.
assessment: Original method sources are present and metadata-verified.

claim: Savitzky-Golay filtering is correctly cited and interpreted.
assessment: Window variation is labeled estimator sensitivity, not a confidence interval.

claim: External iodine dissolution kinetics are used only as conditional directional/scale context.
assessment: The source is not claimed to determine the sustainable-current ceiling of the present electrolyte.

claim: UV-vis and EIS precedents support a proposed validation route for dissolved I3- and interfacial impedance.
assessment: The text proposes measurements in related systems and does not claim that current model states are experimentally validated.

claim: Files labeled NH4Cl are in fact NH4Br, while raw filenames, bytes and hashes remain unchanged.
assessment: The only two active-TeX NH4Cl tokens are explicit correction notes; all 19 active figure PDFs are free of NH4Cl. ZIFB positive-electrode physics remains the manuscript focus; NH4Br is only a representative supporting electrolyte/ligand.
minimum_safe_fix: Carry EXP-META-001 into the immutable release without renaming or altering raw files.

claim: Derivative landmarks did not set Qs/Qf, and the overlay is not independent validation.
assessment: Qf comes from the pre-existing voltage-calibrated branch; the same full-cell record is separately used for the S6 reduced fit. The dependence is now explicit.

claim: The continuum model identity and porous-flow setting are accurately reported.
assessment: COMSOL 6.3.0.290, Tertiary Current Distribution/Nernst-Planck, Free and Porous Media Flow, and porous-domain flowModelType=darcian match the local evidence.
minimum_safe_fix: Publish model identity, hashes and study/solution/dataset metadata in the release.

claim: R_theta, pore transmission and native bare-area identities are not conflated.
assessment: R_theta=1-theta_cal; T_pore=sqrt(K/K0); A_bare/A0=R_theta*T_pore. Qf,cal is defined by mean theta_cal=0.5, not by the product reaching one half.

claim: The matched-control voltage penalty is reproducible and changes only accessibility expressions.
assessment: A 7776-element rtol=3e-4 comparison gives Qs=82.8903/82.8924, endpoint 1.7284/1.4402 V, and delta=-288.156 mV.

claim: Single-I2 values are BSSE-corrected adsorption energies; the two-I2 compact/separated result is a separate electronic-energy diagnostic.
assessment: Text, table, Fig. 5 and Fig. S7 now use consistent energy semantics. CP2K output headers support version 2026.1.
minimum_safe_fix: Include exact inputs, outputs, version and hashes in the immutable release.

claim: MD software identity, trajectory count and within-trajectory uncertainty language are accurate.
assessment: GROMACS 2026.0-conda_forge; one 20 ns trajectory per SOC/charge parameterization; four 5 ns blocks measure within-trajectory stability and are not replicas or SEM. Exact topologies/ITPs/MDPs exist locally.
minimum_safe_fix: Copy the exact topology and analysis package into the immutable release.

claim: Censored current-scan points and cross-evidence sensitivity comparisons are stated without interpolation or false ranking.
assessment: 20/40 mA cm-2 run to Q=120; 80/120 use Q=40 window; 80 reports only Qs>40 and 120 crosses at 8.391. SI notes gamma_salt=2-4 has larger span but is an E-LIT/PRES prior.

claim: Active figures preserve the audited scientific semantics.
assessment: Fig. 5c labels geometric A/A0; Fig. S7 labels relative BSSE-corrected adsorption energy; Fig. S11 separates geometric A/A0, R_theta comparator and native A_bare/A0; no active PDF contains NH4Cl.

claim: Figure and manuscript fonts match the TeX Gyre Termes manuscript family without forbidden fallbacks.
assessment: All 21 PDFs pass: TeX Gyre Termes present; no Arial, Helvetica, DejaVu, Liberation, Calibri, Times New Roman or other unexpected figure family; all fonts embedded and subset; no Type 3 fonts. Non-empty counts are enforced in both canonical and release layouts; failures=[].

claim: Frozen PDFs are fresh and compile without citation/reference/layout warnings.
assessment: Main PDF is 17 pages and SI PDF is 26 pages; each is newer than its TeX source. No undefined citation/reference, LaTeX/package warning, overfull/underfull box, missing character or oversized-float warning was found.
minimum_safe_fix: Recompile and re-run all gates after metadata insertion.

claim: The exact planned tagged-directory URL is embedded and no repository placeholder remains.
assessment: Both sources and PDFs point to r582-zifb-positive-electrode-reproducibility-v1/R582_PUBLICATION_REPRODUCIBILITY; no DOI is invented. The URL becomes publication-valid only after the tag resolves publicly.
minimum_safe_fix: Publish the approved tag/release, open the URL without a private login, and verify its commit/tree. Retain the no-DOI statement unless a DOI is actually issued.

claim: A bounded R582 README and R582-only candidate exist locally, while the target remote tag/release is not yet published.
assessment: The local README centers the porous ZIFB positive electrode and treats NH4Br only as the representative supporting electrolyte. The target tag was absent from git ls-remote at this audit snapshot.
minimum_safe_fix: Push/merge the approved commit, publish r582-zifb-positive-electrode-reproducibility-v1, and verify public immutable resolution.

claim: Author, affiliation, CRediT, funding and acknowledgement fields remain placeholders.
assessment: These fields require author confirmation and cannot be inferred from project evidence.
minimum_safe_fix: Authors must supply and approve the final metadata before submission.

claim: The local candidate contains all 6 main and 13 SI figures, 17 source/QA bundles, renderers/manifests, and EXP-META-001.
assessment: The independent verifier passes the local candidate; refreshed audit files still require one final rebuild into the candidate before publication.
minimum_safe_fix: Rerun the canonical builder and verifier after the audit refresh, then publish the approved snapshot.

claim: The local candidate contains 34 adopted single-I2 CP2K input/output/geometry identity records.
assessment: Exact adopted identities, versions and hashes are packaged locally without upgrading the calculations to solution free energies or nucleation kinetics.
minimum_safe_fix: Verify the record count and hashes from the published tag.

claim: The local candidate contains exact topology, ITP, MDP, protocol, metadata and log identities for all ten reported MD cases.
assessment: Local completeness and manifest checks pass; the package is not yet publicly frozen.
minimum_safe_fix: Verify the manifest and a representative analysis from a clean download after tag publication.

claim: Registered COMSOL identities, scripts, exports and the availability boundary are packaged locally; original .mph files are excluded and unchanged.
assessment: The lightweight package records COMSOL 6.3.0.290 and study/solution/dataset identities without moving original solved models.
minimum_safe_fix: Verify the access note and hashes from the published tag; never move or alter original .mph files.

claim: The current release-identity insertion has been recompiled, rehashed and re-audited.
assessment: Main/SI are 17/26 pages; logs, citation keys, DOI identity, fonts, captions, PDF freshness, hyperlinks and changed-page rendering pass for the current hashes.
minimum_safe_fix: If human author/disclosure metadata later changes TeX/PDF, rerun every gate and use a new versioned tag rather than moving an immutable tag.
